{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d46e706514f04b93a41b6406","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Generic parser traits and interfaces\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core parser traits that different parsing\n4\t//! strategies (YAML, JSON, etc.) can implement.\n5\t\n6\tuse std::path::Path;\n7\tuse crate::parsers::yaml::ParseError as YamlParseError;\n8\t\n9\t/// Generic parser trait for different parsing strategies\n10\t///\n11\t/// This trait defines the core interface for parsers that transform\n12\t/// input of one type into structured output. It is designed to be:\n13\t///\n14\t/// 1. **Generic** - Works with any input/output type combination\n15\t/// 2. **Composable** - Multiple parsers can be chained together\n16\t/// 3. **Extensible** - Supports streaming and incremental parsing\n17\t/// 4. **Error-aware** - Uses structured error types for consistent error handling\n18\t///\n19\t/// # Type Parameters\n20\t///\n21\t/// * `Input` - The input source type (e.g., `&str`, `&[u8]`, `&Path`)\n22\t/// * `Output` - The parsed result type (e.g., configuration struct, AST)\n23\t///\n24\t/// # Trait Bounds\n25\t///\n26\t/// For common use cases, the following type combinations are typical:\n27\t///\n28\t/// - `Input = &str` for string-based formats (YAML, JSON, TOML)\n29\t/// - `Input = &[u8]` for binary formats\n30\t/// - `Input = &Path` for file-based parsing (delegates to string parsing)\n31\t///\n32\t/// The `Output` type is the target representation after parsing.\n33\t///\n34\t/// # Core Method\n35\t///\n36\t/// The [`Parser::parse()`] method is the core parsing operation:\n37\t///\n38\t/// ```ignore\n39\t/// fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n40\t/// ```\n41\t///\n42\t/// # Examples\n43\t///\n44\t/// ## Basic String Parsing\n45\t///\n46\t/// ```ignore\n47\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n48\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n49\t///\n50\t/// struct ConfigParser;\n51\t///\n52\t/// struct Config {\n53\t///     name: String,\n54\t///     value: i32,\n55\t/// }\n56\t///\n57\t/// impl Parser<&str, Config> for ConfigParser {\n58\t///     fn parse(&self, source: &str) -> Result<Config, ParseError> {\n59\t///         // Parse logic here\n60\t///         Ok(Config { name: \"test\".to_string(), value: 42 })\n61\t///     }\n62\t/// }\n63\t///\n64\t/// let parser = ConfigParser;\n65\t/// let config = parser.parse(\"name: test\\nvalue: 42\")?;\n66\t/// ```\n67\t///\n68\t/// ## Chaining Parsers\n69\t///\n70\t/// ```ignore\n71\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n72\t///\n73\t/// // Parser 1: Raw string -> Intermediate representation\n74\t/// struct FirstParser;\n75\t/// impl Parser<&str, Intermediate> for FirstParser { /* ... */ }\n76\t///\n77\t/// // Parser 2: Intermediate -> Final output\n78\t/// struct SecondParser;\n79\t/// impl Parser<Intermediate, Output> for SecondParser { /* ... */ }\n80\t///\n81\t/// let first = FirstParser;\n82\t/// let second = SecondParser;\n83\t/// let output = second.parse(first.parse(input)?);\n84\t/// ```\n85\t///\n86\t/// ## Error Handling\n87\t///\n88\t/// ```ignore\n89\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n90\t///\n91\t/// match parser.parse(source) {\n92\t///     Ok(output) => println!(\"Parsed successfully: {:?}\", output),\n93\t///     Err(ParseError::Syntax(msg)) => eprintln!(\"Syntax error: {}\", msg),\n94\t///     Err(ParseError::Io(msg)) => eprintln!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n95\t///     Err(e) => eprintln!(\"Other error: {}\", e),\n96\t/// }\n97\t/// ```\n98\tpub trait Parser<Input, Output> {\n99\t    /// Parse input and return the parsed result\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// This method accepts input of type `Input` and attempts to parse it\n102\t    /// into a structured `Output` type. Returns `Ok(Output)` on success,\n103\t    /// or `Err(ParseError)` if parsing fails.\n104\t    ///\n105\t    /// # Arguments\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// # Returns\n110\t    ///\n111\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n112\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n113\t    ///\n114\t    /// # Errors\n115\t    ///\n116\t    /// This method will return an error if:\n117\t    /// - The input syntax is invalid (syntax error)\n118\t    /// - The input cannot be read (I/O error)\n119\t    /// - The input violates constraints (validation error)\n120\t    /// - Types don't match expectations (type mismatch)\n121\t    ///\n122\t    /// # Examples\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// ```ignore\n125\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n126\t    ///\n127\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n128\t    /// let result = parser.parse(\"key: value\")?;\n129\t    /// ```\n130\t    fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n131\t\n132\t    /// Parse input with extended options\n133\t    ///\n134\t    /// This method provides a way to customize parsing behavior through\n135\t    /// options. The default implementation delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// # Arguments\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n140\t    /// * `options` - Parsing options to customize behavior\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// # Returns\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n145\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// # Examples\n148\t    ///\n149\t    /// ```ignore\n150\t    /// use armor::parsers::{Parser, ParseOptions};\n151\t    ///\n152\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n153\t    /// let options = ParseOptions {\n154\t    ///     strict_mode: true,\n155\t    ///     preserve_comments: false,\n156\t    ///     ..Default::default()\n157\t    /// };\n158\t    /// let result = parser.parse_with_options(source, options)?;\n159\t    /// ```\n160\t    fn parse_with_options(&self, source: Input, options: ParseOptions) -> Result<Output, ParseError> {\n161\t        let _ = options; // Ignore options in default implementation\n162\t        self.parse(source)\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Parse a file at the given path\n166\t    ///\n167\t    /// Convenience method that reads a file and parses its contents.\n168\t    /// The default implementation reads the file and delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n169\t    ///\n170\t    /// # Arguments\n171\t    ///\n172\t    /// * `path` - Path to the file to parse\n173\t    ///\n174\t    /// # Returns\n175\t    ///\n176\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n177\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - I/O or parsing error\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// # Errors\n180\t    ///\n181\t    /// This method will return an error if:\n182\t    /// - The file cannot be read (I/O error)\n183\t    /// - The file contents are invalid (parsing error)\n184\t    ///\n185\t    /// # Examples\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// ```ignore\n188\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n189\t    /// use std::path::Path;\n190\t    ///\n191\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n192\t    /// let result = parser.parse_file(Path::new(\"config.yaml\"))?;\n193\t    /// ```\n194\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n195\t    where\n196\t        Self: Sized,\n197\t        Input: From<String>,\n198\t    {\n199\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n200\t            .map_err(|e| ParseError::Io(e.to_string()))?;\n201\t        self.parse(content.into())\n202\t    }\n203\t\n204\t    /// Validate input without fully parsing\n205\t    ///\n206\t    /// This method performs lightweight validation to check if the input\n207\t    /// is well-formed without constructing the full output structure.\n208\t    /// The default implementation attempts parsing and discards the result.\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Arguments\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// * `source` - The input source to validate\n213\t    ///\n214\t    /// # Returns\n215\t    ///\n216\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Input is valid\n217\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Input is invalid\n218\t    ///\n219\t    /// # Examples\n220\t    ///\n221\t    /// ```ignore\n222\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n223\t    ///\n224\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n225\t    /// if parser.validate(\"key: value\").is_ok() {\n226\t    ///     println!(\"Input is valid!\");\n227\t    /// }\n228\t    /// ```\n229\t    fn validate(&self, source: Input) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n230\t        self.parse(source)?;\n231\t        Ok(())\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get metadata about the parser\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// Returns information about the parser's capabilities and configuration.\n237\t    ///\n238\t    /// # Returns\n239\t    ///\n240\t    /// Parser metadata including supported formats and features\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// # Examples\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// ```ignore\n245\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n248\t    /// let metadata = parser.metadata();\n249\t    /// println!(\"Parser: {}\", metadata.name());\n250\t    /// println!(\"Supports streaming: {}\", metadata.supports_streaming());\n251\t    /// ```\n252\t    fn metadata(&self) -> ParseMetadata {\n253\t        ParseMetadata::default()\n254\t    }\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t/// Trait for parsers that support streaming input\n258\t///\n259\t/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with capabilities for processing large\n260\t/// inputs as a stream of chunks or multiple sources.\n261\t///\n262\t/// # Examples\n263\t///\n264\t/// ```ignore\n265\t/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, StreamingParser};\n266\t///\n267\t/// let parser = MyStreamingParser::new();\n268\t///\n269\t/// // Parse multiple sources\n270\t/// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n271\t/// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n272\t///\n273\t/// for result in results {\n274\t///     println!(\"Parsed: {:?}\", result);\n275\t/// }\n276\t/// ```\n277\tpub trait StreamingParser<Input, Output>: Parser<Input, Output> {\n278\t    /// Parse multiple sources in sequence\n279\t    ///\n280\t    /// This method processes a collection of input sources and returns\n281\t    /// a vector of parsed results. Errors from individual sources are\n282\t    /// collected and returned together.\n283\t    ///\n284\t    /// # Arguments\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// # Returns\n289\t    ///\n290\t    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n291\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// # Examples\n294\t    ///\n295\t    /// ```ignore\n296\t    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n297\t    ///\n298\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n299\t    /// let sources = vec![\"config1.yaml\", \"config2.yaml\"];\n300\t    /// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n301\t    /// ```\n302\t    fn parse_stream<'a, I>(\n303\t        &self,\n304\t        sources: I,\n305\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n306\t    where\n307\t        Input: 'a,\n308\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>;\n309\t\n310\t    /// Parse sources in parallel\n311\t    ///\n312\t    /// This method processes multiple sources concurrently using parallel\n313\t    /// execution. Results are collected in the order of the input iterator.\n314\t    ///\n315\t    /// # Arguments\n316\t    ///\n317\t    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n318\t    /// * `parallelism` - Maximum number of concurrent parse operations\n319\t    ///\n320\t    /// # Returns\n321\t    ///\n322\t    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n323\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n324\t    ///\n325\t    /// # Examples\n326\t    ///\n327\t    /// ```ignore\n328\t    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n329\t    ///\n330\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n331\t    /// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n332\t    /// let results = parser.parse_parallel(sources, 4)?;\n333\t    /// ```\n334\t    fn parse_parallel<'a, I>(\n335\t        &self,\n336\t        sources: I,\n337\t        parallelism: usize,\n338\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n339\t    where\n340\t        Input: 'a,\n341\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>,\n342\t    {\n343\t        // Default implementation processes sequentially\n344\t        self.parse_stream(sources)\n345\t    }\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t/// Trait for parsers that support incremental parsing\n349\t///\n350\t/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with the ability to parse input in chunks,\n351\t/// allowing processing of large inputs without loading the entire source\n352\t/// into memory.\n353\t///\n354\t/// # Examples\n355\t///\n356\t/// ```ignore\n357\t/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, IncrementalParser};\n358\t///\n359\t/// let parser = MyIncrementalParser::new();\n360\t///\n361\t/// // Initialize parsing\n362\t/// parser.init_parse()?;\n363\t///\n364\t/// // Feed chunks incrementally\n365\t/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"key: \")?;\n366\t/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"value\")?;\n367\t///\n368\t/// // Complete parsing and get result\n369\t/// let result = parser.finalize_parse()?;\n370\t/// ```\n371\tpub trait IncrementalParser<Output>: Parser<Vec<u8>, Output> {\n372\t    /// Initialize incremental parsing\n373\t    ///\n374\t    /// Called before feeding chunks. Sets up internal state for parsing.\n375\t    ///\n376\t    /// # Returns\n377\t    ///\n378\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Parser initialized successfully\n379\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Initialization failed\n380\t    fn init_parse(&mut self) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n381\t\n382\t    /// Feed a chunk of input to the parser\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// Processes a chunk of input without completing the parse. Can be\n385\t    /// called multiple times before finalization.\n386\t    ///\n387\t    /// # Arguments\n388\t    ///\n389\t    /// * `chunk` - Chunk of input data\n390\t    ///\n391\t    /// # Returns\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Chunk processed successfully\n394\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Chunk processing failed\n395\t    fn feed_chunk(&mut self, chunk: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n396\t\n397\t    /// Finalize parsing and return the result\n398\t    ///\n399\t    /// Called after all chunks have been fed. Completes the parse and\n400\t    /// returns the final result.\n401\t    ///\n402\t    /// # Returns\n403\t    ///\n404\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Parsing completed successfully\n405\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Finalization or parsing failed\n406\t    fn finalize_parse(&mut self) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n407\t}\n408\t\n409\t/// Options for customizing parser behavior\n410\t///\n411\t/// This structure provides configuration options that can be passed to\n412\t/// [`Parser::parse_with_options()`].\n413\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n414\tpub struct ParseOptions {\n415\t    /// Enable strict mode (disallow unknown fields, enforce types strictly)\n416\t    pub strict_mode: bool,\n417\t    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n418\t    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n419\t    /// Allow duplicate keys (if format normally disallows them)\n420\t    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n421\t    /// Maximum depth for nested structures (0 = no limit)\n422\t    pub max_depth: usize,\n423\t    /// Custom delimiter for key-value pairs (None = use format default)\n424\t    pub delimiter: Option<char>,\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\timpl ParseOptions {\n428\t    /// Create new parse options with default values\n429\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n430\t        Self::default()\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    /// Create options for strict parsing\n434\t    ///\n435\t    /// Sets `strict_mode` to true and `allow_duplicates` to false.\n436\t    ///\n437\t    /// # Examples\n438\t    ///\n439\t    /// ```\n440\t    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n441\t    ///\n442\t    /// let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n443\t    /// assert!(options.strict_mode);\n444\t    /// assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n445\t    /// ```\n446\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n447\t        Self {\n448\t            strict_mode: true,\n449\t            allow_duplicates: false,\n450\t            ..Default::default()\n451\t        }\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    /// Create options for lenient parsing\n455\t    ///\n456\t    /// Sets `strict_mode` to false and `allow_duplicates` to false,\n470\t            allow_duplicates: true,\n471\t            ..Default::default()\n472\t        }\n473\t    }\n474\t\n475\t    /// Builder method: set strict mode\n476\t    pub fn about a parser's capabilities\n507\t///\n508\t/// This structure describes what a parser can do, what formats it supports,\n509\t/// and its current configuration.\n510\t#[derive(Debug, supports_incremental: false,\n534\t            extensions: Vec::new(),\n535\t            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n536\t        }\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    /// Builder method: indicate {\n634\t        Self::Syntax(msg.into())\n635\t    }\n636\t\n637\t    /// Create an I/O error\n638\t    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n639\t        Self::Io(msg.into())\n640\t    }\n641\t\n642\t    /// Create a validation error\n643\t    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n644\t        Self::Validation(msg.into())\n645\t    }\n646\t\n647\t    /// Create a type impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n658\t        Self::Other(msg.into())\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n662\t    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n663\t        matches!(self, Self::Syntax(_))\n664\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_syntax())\n665\t    }\n666\t\n667\t    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n668\t    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n669\t        matches!(self, Self::Io(_))\n670\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_io())\n671\t    }\n672\t\n673\t    /// Check if this is a validation error\n674\t    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n675\t        matches!(self, Self::Validation(_))\n676\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_validation())\n677\t    }\n678\t\n679\t    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n680\t    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n681\t        matches!(self, Self::TypeMismatch { .. })\n682\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_type_mismatch())\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    /// Get a brief summary of the error\n686\t    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n687\t        match self {\n688\t            Self::Yaml(e) => e.summary(),\n689\t            Self::Io(msg) => format!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n690\t            Self::Validation(msg) => format!(\"validation error: {}\", msg),\n691\t            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n692\t                format!(\"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n693\t            }\n694\t            Self::Syntax(msg) => format!(\"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n695\t            Self::Other(msg) => format!(\"error: {}\", msg),\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\timpl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {\n701\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n702\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())\n703\t    }\n704\t}\n705\t\n706\timpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n707\t\n708\timpl From<YamlParseError> for ParseError {\n709\t    fn from(err: YamlParseError) -> Self {\n710\t        Self::Yaml(err)\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\timpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n715\t    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n716\t        Self::Io(err.to_string())\n717\t    }\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t#[cfg(test)]\n721\tmod tests {\n722\t    use }\n825\t}\n826\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6fa90fff-4ed0-4651-8041-4c40b9dccd9a","uuid":"158ec7e3-b437-4e3c-8233-176b899e5392","timestamp":"2026-07-11T14:35:31.315Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/traits.rs","content":"//! Generic parser traits and interfaces\n//!\n//! This module defines the core parser traits that different parsing\n//! strategies (YAML, JSON, etc.) can implement.\n\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::ParseError as YamlParseError;\n\n/// Generic parser trait for different parsing strategies\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsers that transform\n/// input of one type into structured output. It is designed to be:\n///\n/// 1. **Generic** - Works with any input/output type combination\n/// 2. **Composable** - Multiple parsers can be chained together\n/// 3. **Extensible** - Supports streaming and incremental parsing\n/// 4. **Error-aware** - Uses structured error types for consistent error handling\n///\n/// # Type Parameters\n///\n/// * `Input` - The input source type (e.g., `&str`, `&[u8]`, `&Path`)\n/// * `Output` - The parsed result type (e.g., configuration struct, AST)\n///\n/// # Trait Bounds\n///\n/// For common use cases, the following type combinations are typical:\n///\n/// - `Input = &str` for string-based formats (YAML, JSON, TOML)\n/// - `Input = &[u8]` for binary formats\n/// - `Input = &Path` for file-based parsing (delegates to string parsing)\n///\n/// The `Output` type is the target representation after parsing.\n///\n/// # Core Method\n///\n/// The [`Parser::parse()`] method is the core parsing operation:\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n/// ```\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Basic String Parsing\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n///\n/// struct ConfigParser;\n///\n/// struct Config {\n///     name: String,\n///     value: i32,\n/// }\n///\n/// impl Parser<&str, Config> for ConfigParser {\n///     fn parse(&self, source: &str) -> Result<Config, ParseError> {\n///         // Parse logic here\n///         Ok(Config { name: \"test\".to_string(), value: 42 })\n///     }\n/// }\n///\n/// let parser = ConfigParser;\n/// let config = parser.parse(\"name: test\\nvalue: 42\")?;\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Chaining Parsers\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n///\n/// // Parser 1: Raw string -> Intermediate representation\n/// struct FirstParser;\n/// impl Parser<&str, Intermediate> for FirstParser { /* ... */ }\n///\n/// // Parser 2: Intermediate -> Final output\n/// struct SecondParser;\n/// impl Parser<Intermediate, Output> for SecondParser { /* ... */ }\n///\n/// let first = FirstParser;\n/// let second = SecondParser;\n/// let output = second.parse(first.parse(input)?);\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Error Handling\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n///\n/// match parser.parse(source) {\n///     Ok(output) => println!(\"Parsed successfully: {:?}\", output),\n///     Err(ParseError::Syntax(msg)) => eprintln!(\"Syntax error: {}\", msg),\n///     Err(ParseError::Io(msg)) => eprintln!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n///     Err(e) => eprintln!(\"Other error: {}\", e),\n/// }\n/// ```\npub trait Parser<Input, Output> {\n    /// Parse input and return the parsed result\n    ///\n    /// This method accepts input of type `Input` and attempts to parse it\n    /// into a structured `Output` type. Returns `Ok(Output)` on success,\n    /// or `Err(ParseError)` if parsing fails.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    ///\n    /// This method will return an error if:\n    /// - The input syntax is invalid (syntax error)\n    /// - The input cannot be read (I/O error)\n    /// - The input violates constraints (validation error)\n    /// - Types don't match expectations (type mismatch)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let result = parser.parse(\"key: value\")?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n\n    /// Parse input with extended options\n    ///\n    /// This method provides a way to customize parsing behavior through\n    /// options. The default implementation delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n    /// * `options` - Parsing options to customize behavior\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::{Parser, ParseOptions};\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let options = ParseOptions {\n    ///     strict_mode: true,\n    ///     preserve_comments: false,\n    ///     ..Default::default()\n    /// };\n    /// let result = parser.parse_with_options(source, options)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_with_options(&self, source: Input, options: ParseOptions) -> Result<Output, ParseError> {\n        let _ = options; // Ignore options in default implementation\n        self.parse(source)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a file at the given path\n    ///\n    /// Convenience method that reads a file and parses its contents.\n    /// The default implementation reads the file and delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `path` - Path to the file to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - I/O or parsing error\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    ///\n    /// This method will return an error if:\n    /// - The file cannot be read (I/O error)\n    /// - The file contents are invalid (parsing error)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    /// use std::path::Path;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let result = parser.parse_file(Path::new(\"config.yaml\"))?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n        Input: From<String>,\n    {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::Io(e.to_string()))?;\n        self.parse(content.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Validate input without fully parsing\n    ///\n    /// This method performs lightweight validation to check if the input\n    /// is well-formed without constructing the full output structure.\n    /// The default implementation attempts parsing and discards the result.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to validate\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Input is valid\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Input is invalid\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// if parser.validate(\"key: value\").is_ok() {\n    ///     println!(\"Input is valid!\");\n    /// }\n    /// ```\n    fn validate(&self, source: Input) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n        self.parse(source)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get metadata about the parser\n    ///\n    /// Returns information about the parser's capabilities and configuration.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// Parser metadata including supported formats and features\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let metadata = parser.metadata();\n    /// println!(\"Parser: {}\", metadata.name());\n    /// println!(\"Supports streaming: {}\", metadata.supports_streaming());\n    /// ```\n    fn metadata(&self) -> ParseMetadata {\n        ParseMetadata::default()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for parsers that support streaming input\n///\n/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with capabilities for processing large\n/// inputs as a stream of chunks or multiple sources.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, StreamingParser};\n///\n/// let parser = MyStreamingParser::new();\n///\n/// // Parse multiple sources\n/// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n/// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n///\n/// for result in results {\n///     println!(\"Parsed: {:?}\", result);\n/// }\n/// ```\npub trait StreamingParser<Input, Output>: Parser<Input, Output> {\n    /// Parse multiple sources in sequence\n    ///\n    /// This method processes a collection of input sources and returns\n    /// a vector of parsed results. Errors from individual sources are\n    /// collected and returned together.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let sources = vec![\"config1.yaml\", \"config2.yaml\"];\n    /// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_stream<'a, I>(\n        &self,\n        sources: I,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n    where\n        Input: 'a,\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>;\n\n    /// Parse sources in parallel\n    ///\n    /// This method processes multiple sources concurrently using parallel\n    /// execution. Results are collected in the order of the input iterator.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n    /// * `parallelism` - Maximum number of concurrent parse operations\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n    /// let results = parser.parse_parallel(sources, 4)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_parallel<'a, I>(\n        &self,\n        sources: I,\n        parallelism: usize,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n    where\n        Input: 'a,\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>,\n    {\n        // Default implementation processes sequentially\n        self.parse_stream(sources)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for parsers that support incremental parsing\n///\n/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with the ability to parse input in chunks,\n/// allowing processing of large inputs without loading the entire source\n/// into memory.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, IncrementalParser};\n///\n/// let parser = MyIncrementalParser::new();\n///\n/// // Initialize parsing\n/// parser.init_parse()?;\n///\n/// // Feed chunks incrementally\n/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"key: \")?;\n/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"value\")?;\n///\n/// // Complete parsing and get result\n/// let result = parser.finalize_parse()?;\n/// ```\npub trait IncrementalParser<Output>: Parser<Vec<u8>, Output> {\n    /// Initialize incremental parsing\n    ///\n    /// Called before feeding chunks. Sets up internal state for parsing.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Parser initialized successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Initialization failed\n    fn init_parse(&mut self) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n\n    /// Feed a chunk of input to the parser\n    ///\n    /// Processes a chunk of input without completing the parse. Can be\n    /// called multiple times before finalization.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `chunk` - Chunk of input data\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Chunk processed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Chunk processing failed\n    fn feed_chunk(&mut self, chunk: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n\n    /// Finalize parsing and return the result\n    ///\n    /// Called after all chunks have been fed. Completes the parse and\n    /// returns the final result.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Parsing completed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Finalization or parsing failed\n    fn finalize_parse(&mut self) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n}\n\n/// Options for customizing parser behavior\n///\n/// This structure provides configuration options that can be passed to\n/// [`Parser::parse_with_options()`].\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ParseOptions {\n    /// Enable strict mode (disallow unknown fields, enforce types strictly)\n    pub strict_mode: bool,\n    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n    /// Allow duplicate keys (if format normally disallows them)\n    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n    /// Maximum depth for nested structures (0 = no limit)\n    pub max_depth: usize,\n    /// Custom delimiter for key-value pairs (None = use format default)\n    pub delimiter: Option<char>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseOptions {\n    /// Create new parse options with default values\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Create options for strict parsing\n    ///\n    /// Sets `strict_mode` to true and `allow_duplicates` to false.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n    ///\n    /// let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n    /// assert!(options.strict_mode);\n    /// assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_mode: true,\n            allow_duplicates: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create options for lenient parsing\n    ///\n    /// Sets `strict_mode` to false and `allow_duplicates` to true.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n    ///\n    /// let options = ParseOptions::lenient();\n    /// assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n    /// assert!(options.allow_duplicates);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_mode: false,\n            allow_duplicates: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set strict mode\n    pub fn with_strict_mode(mut self, strict: bool) -> Self {\n        self.strict_mode = strict;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set comment preservation\n    pub fn with_preserve_comments(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n        self.preserve_comments = preserve;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set duplicate key allowance\n    pub fn with_allow_duplicates(mut self, allow: bool) -> Self {\n        self.allow_duplicates = allow;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set maximum nesting depth\n    pub fn with_max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self {\n        self.max_depth = depth;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set custom delimiter\n    pub fn with_delimiter(mut self, delimiter: char) -> Self {\n        self.delimiter = Some(delimiter);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata about a parser's capabilities\n///\n/// This structure describes what a parser can do, what formats it supports,\n/// and its current configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseMetadata {\n    /// Human-readable name of the parser (e.g., \"YAML Parser\", \"JSON Parser\")\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Format name this parser handles (e.g., \"YAML 1.2\", \"JSON\")\n    pub format: String,\n    /// Whether the parser supports streaming operations\n    pub supports_streaming: bool,\n    /// Whether the parser supports incremental parsing\n    pub supports_incremental: bool,\n    /// Supported file extensions (e.g., [\"yaml\", \"yml\"])\n    pub extensions: Vec<String>,\n    /// Parser version\n    pub version: String,\n}\n\nimpl ParseMetadata {\n    /// Create new parser metadata\n    pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>, format: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            name: name.into(),\n            format: format.into(),\n            supports_streaming: false,\n            supports_incremental: false,\n            extensions: Vec::new(),\n            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: indicate streaming support\n    pub fn with_streaming(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n        self.supports_streaming = supports;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: indicate incremental parsing support\n    pub fn with_incremental(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n        self.supports_incremental = supports;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: add supported file extension\n    pub fn with_extension(mut self, ext: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.extensions.push(ext.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set parser version\n    pub fn with_version(mut self, version: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.version = version.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the parser name\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.name\n    }\n\n    /// Get the format name\n    pub fn format(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.format\n    }\n\n    /// Check if streaming is supported\n    pub fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n        self.supports_streaming\n    }\n\n    /// Check if incremental parsing is supported\n    pub fn supports_incremental(&self) -> bool {\n        self.supports_incremental\n    }\n\n    /// Get supported file extensions\n    pub fn extensions(&self) -> &[String] {\n        &self.extensions\n    }\n\n    /// Get parser version\n    pub fn version(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.version\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ParseMetadata {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            name: \"Generic Parser\".to_string(),\n            format: \"Unknown\".to_string(),\n            supports_streaming: false,\n            supports_incremental: false,\n            extensions: Vec::new(),\n            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Unified parse error type\n///\n/// This type wraps format-specific errors (e.g., `yaml::ParseError`)\n/// into a common type that the generic `Parser` trait can use.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum ParseError {\n    /// YAML parsing error\n    Yaml(YamlParseError),\n    /// I/O error\n    Io(String),\n    /// Validation error\n    Validation(String),\n    /// Type mismatch error\n    TypeMismatch {\n        field: String,\n        expected: String,\n        actual: String,\n    },\n    /// Syntax error\n    Syntax(String),\n    /// Other error\n    Other(String),\n}\n\nimpl ParseError {\n    /// Create a syntax error\n    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Syntax(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create an I/O error\n    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Io(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a validation error\n    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Validation(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a type mismatch error\n    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::TypeMismatch {\n            field: field.into(),\n            expected: expected.into(),\n            actual: actual.into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create an other error\n    pub fn other(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Other(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Syntax(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_syntax())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Io(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_io())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a validation error\n    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Validation(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_validation())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::TypeMismatch { .. })\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_type_mismatch())\n    }\n\n    /// Get a brief summary of the error\n    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n        match self {\n            Self::Yaml(e) => e.summary(),\n            Self::Io(msg) => format!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Validation(msg) => format!(\"validation error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n                format!(\"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n            }\n            Self::Syntax(msg) => format!(\"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Other(msg) => format!(\"error: {}\", msg),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\nimpl From<YamlParseError> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: YamlParseError) -> Self {\n        Self::Yaml(err)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n        Self::Io(err.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_default() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::default();\n        assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(!options.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n        assert_eq!(options.max_depth, 0);\n        assert!(options.delimiter.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_strict() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n        assert!(options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_lenient() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::lenient();\n        assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(options.allow_duplicates);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_builder() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::new()\n            .with_strict_mode(true)\n            .with_preserve_comments(true)\n            .with_allow_duplicates(false)\n            .with_max_depth(10)\n            .with_delimiter('=');\n\n        assert!(options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(options.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n        assert_eq!(options.max_depth, 10);\n        assert_eq!(options.delimiter, Some('='));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_metadata_default() {\n        let metadata = ParseMetadata::default();\n        assert_eq!(metadata.name, \"Generic Parser\");\n        assert_eq!(metadata.format, \"Unknown\");\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_streaming);\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_incremental);\n        assert!(metadata.extensions.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(metadata.version, \"1.0.0\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_metadata_builder() {\n        let metadata = ParseMetadata::new(\"YAML Parser\", \"YAML 1.2\")\n            .with_streaming(true)\n            .with_incremental(false)\n            .with_extension(\"yaml\")\n            .with_extension(\"yml\")\n            .with_version(\"2.0.0\");\n\n        assert_eq!(metadata.name(), \"YAML Parser\");\n        assert_eq!(metadata.format(), \"YAML 1.2\");\n        assert!(metadata.supports_streaming());\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_incremental());\n        assert_eq!(metadata.extensions().len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(metadata.version(), \"2.0.0\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_creation() {\n        let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n        assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n\n        let io_err = ParseError::io(\"test\");\n        assert!(io_err.is_io());\n\n        let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"test\");\n        assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n\n        let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"int\", \"string\");\n        assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_summary() {\n        let err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\");\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"syntax error\"));\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"invalid token\"));\n\n        let err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"type mismatch\"));\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"port\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_display() {\n        let err = ParseError::validation(\"value out of range\");\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", err);\n        assert!(display.contains(\"validation error\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"value out of range\"));\n    }\n}\n","numLines":826,"startLine":1,"totalLines":826}}}

ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) ->            preserve_quotes: false,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n Result types for YAML parsing operations\n//!\n//! This module defines the result types used by the YAML parser.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::error::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum Status {\n    /// Operation completed successfully\n    SUCCESS,\n    /// Operation encountered an error\n    ERROR,\n}\n\nimpl Status {\n    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if status is ERROR\n    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n        if success {\n            Status::SUCCESS\n        } else {\n            Status::ERROR\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to boolean\n    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n        self.is_success()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Status {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n///\n/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct OperationResult<T> {\n    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    pub status: Status,\n    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n    pub data: Option<T>,\n    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self { status, data, error }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n            data: Some(data),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::ERROR,\n            data: None,\n            error: Some(message),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_success()\n    }\n\n }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseResult<T> {\n    /// The parsed value, if successful\n    value: Option<T>,\n    /// The error, if parsing failed\n    error: Option<ParseError>,\n    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n}\n\nimpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n    /// Create a successful parse result\n    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: Some(value),\n            error: None,\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed parse result\n    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: None,\n            error: Some(error),\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse failed\n    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the parsed value\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n        self.value.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the error, if any\n    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n        self.error.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n        &self.metadata\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the parse failed\n    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n        self.value.expect(\n            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n    pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T {\n        self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n    }\n\n    /// Map the success value to a new type\n    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        match self.value {\n            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n                value: Some(f(v)),\n                error: None,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n            },\n            None => ParseResult {\n                value: None,\n                error: self.error,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n        match result {\n            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ParseMetadata {\n    /// Number of lines processed\n    pub if known\n    pub source_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseMetadata {\n    /// Create new metadata\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Set the number of lines self, bytes: usize) -> Self {\n        self.bytes_processed = bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the source path\n    pub fn with_source(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.source_path = Some(path.into());\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML validation operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationResult {\n    /// Whether validation passed\n }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed validation result\n    pub fn failure(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            valid: false,\n            errors,\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if validation passed\n    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n        self.valid && self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any errors\n    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any warnings\n    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n This module provides comprehensive configuration options for controlling\n4\t//! parsing behavior across different parser implementations. It defines:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! - [`ParserConfig`] - Main configuration struct for all parsing options\n7\t//! - [`ParserMode`] - Strict vs lenient parsing modes\n8\t//! - [`TypeConstructor`] - Custom hooks for type construction\n9\t//! - Builder pattern for fluent configuration\n10\t\n11\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n12\tuse std::fmt;\n13\t\n14\t/// Parser execution mode\n15\t///\n16\t/// Defines the strictness level for parsing operations. Each mode has\n17\t/// specific behaviors for handling malformed input, unknown fields, and\n18\t/// type mismatches.\n19\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n20\tpub enum ParserMode {\n21\t    /// Strict mode - reject any malformed or unexpected input\n22\t    ///\n23\t    /// In strict mode:\n24\t    /// - Unknown fields cause parsing to fail\n25\t    /// - Type mismatches are errors (not coerced)\n26\t    /// - Duplicate keys are rejected\n27\t    /// - All syntax rules are enforced\n28\t    /// - Missing required fields cause errors\n29\t    Strict,\n30\t\n31\t    /// Lenient mode - attempt to recover from errors\n32\t    ///\n33\t    /// In lenient mode:\n34\t    /// - Unknown fields are silently ignored\n35\t    /// - Type mismatches are coerced when possible (e.g., string → number)\n36\t    /// - Last duplicate key wins (with warning if configured)\n37\t    /// - Some syntax variations are accepted\n38\t    /// - Missing optional fields use defaults\n39\t    Lenient,\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\timpl ParserMode {\n43\t    /// Check if this mode is strict\n44\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n45\t        matches!(self, Self::Strict)\n46\t    }\n47\t\n48\t    /// Check if this mode is lenient\n49\t    pub fn is_lenient(&self) -> bool {\n50\t        matches!(self, Self::Lenient)\n51\t    }\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl fmt::Display for ParserMode {\n55\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n56\t        match self {\n57\t            Self::Strict => write!(f, \"strict\"),\n58\t            Self::Lenient => write!(f, \"lenient\"),\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\timpl Default for ParserMode {\n64\t    fn default() -> Self {\n65\t        // Default to lenient mode for better user experience\n66\t        Self::Lenient\n67\t    }\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t/// Type constructor function signature\n71\t///\n72\t/// This function type defines the signature for custom type constructors.\n73\t/// It takes a field name and raw value, and attempts to construct the\n74\t/// target type from those inputs.\n75\tpub type TypeConstructorFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String>;\n76\t\n77\t/// Custom type constructor hook\n78\t///\n79\t/// Allows users to register custom logic for constructing specific types\n80\t/// during parsing. This is useful for:\n81\t///\n82\t/// - Custom enum parsing (e.g., \"warn\" → LogLevel::Warning)\n83\t/// - Validation-rich construction (e.g., ensure ports are in range)\n84\t/// - Complex type assembly (e.g., Duration from \"5s\" string)\n85\t/// - Default value injection for optional fields\n86\t///\n87\t/// # Examples\n88\t///\n89\t/// ```\n90\t/// use armor::parsers::config::TypeConstructor;\n91\t///\n92\t/// // Custom constructor for log levels\n93\t/// fn log_level_constructor(\n94\t///     field: &str,\n95\t///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n96\t/// ) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n97\t///     let s = value.as_str()\n98\t///         .ok_or(\"expected string\")?\n99\t///         .to_lowercase();\n100\t///\n101\t///     let level = match s.as_str() {\n102\t///         \"debug\" => 0,\n103\t///         \"info\" => 1,\n104\t///         \"warn\" | \"warning\" => 2,\n105\t///         \"error\" => 3,\n106\t///         _ => return Err(format!(\"invalid log level: {}\", s)),\n107\t///     };\n108\t///\n109\t///     Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Number(level.into()))\n110\t/// }\n111\t///\n112\t/// let constructor = TypeConstructor::new(\"LogLevel\", log_level_constructor);\n113\t/// ```\n114\t#[derive(Clone)]\n115\tpub struct TypeConstructor {\n116\t    /// Name of the type this constructor handles (for debugging)\n117\t    pub type_name: String,\n118\t    /// The constructor function\n119\t    pub constructor: TypeConstructorFn,\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\timpl TypeConstructor {\n123\t    /// Create a new type constructor\n124\t    ///\n125\t    /// # Arguments\n126\t    ///\n127\t    /// * `type_name` - Human-readable name for the type being constructed\n128\t    /// * `constructor` - Function that implements the construction logic\n129\t    pub fn new(type_name: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructorFn) -> Self {\n130\t        Self {\n131\t            type_name: type_name.into(),\n132\t            constructor,\n133\t        }\n134\t    }\n135\t\n136\t    /// Invoke the constructor\n137\t    ///\n138\t    /// Calls the underlying constructor function with the provided inputs.\n139\t    ///\n140\t    /// # Arguments\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// * `field` - Field name being constructed\n143\t    /// * `value` - Raw value from the parser\n144\t    ///\n145\t    /// # Returns\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// * `Ok(serde_yaml::Value)` - Successfully constructed value\n148\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Construction error message\n149\t    pub fn construct(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n150\t        (self.constructor)(field, value)\n151\t    }\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\timpl fmt::Debug for TypeConstructor {\n155\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n156\t        f.debug_struct(\"TypeConstructor\")\n157\t            .field(\"type_name\", &self.type_name)\n158\t            .field(\"constructor\", &\"<function>\")\n159\t            .finish()\n160\t    }\n161\t}\n162\t\n163\t/// Validation hook function signature\n164\t///\n165\t/// This function type defines the signature for custom validation hooks.\n166\t/// It takes a field name and parsed value, and validates that the value\n167\t/// meets application-specific constraints.\n168\tpub type ValidationFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String>;\n169\t\n170\t/// Custom validation hook\n171\t///\n172\t/// Allows users to register custom validation logic for specific fields\n173\t/// or types. Validation runs after parsing but before the final result\n174\t/// is returned.\n175\t///\n176\t/// # Examples\n177\t///\n178\t/// ```\n179\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidationHook;\n180\t///\n181\t/// // Validate port numbers are in valid range\n182\t/// fn validate_port(\n183\t///     field: &str,\n184\t///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n185\t/// ) -> Result<(), String> {\n186\t///     let port = value.as_i64()\n187\t///         .ok_or(\"port must be an integer\")?;\n188\t///\n189\t///     if !(1..=65535).contains(&port) {\n190\t///         return Err(format!(\"port {} out of valid range (1-65535)\", port));\n191\t///     }\n192\t///\n193\t///     Ok(())\n194\t/// }\n195\t///\n196\t/// let hook = ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port);\n197\t/// ```\n198\t#[derive(Clone)]\n199\tpub struct ValidationHook {\n200\t    /// Pattern for matching field names (supports simple \"*\"\" wildcard)\n201\t    pub field_pattern: String,\n202\t    /// The validation function\n203\t    pub validator: ValidationFn,\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\timpl ValidationHook {\n207\t    /// Create a new validation hook\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// # Arguments\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// * `field_pattern` - Pattern to match field names (supports \"*\"\" wildcard)\n212\t    /// * `validator` - Function that implements the validation logic\n213\t    pub fn new(field_pattern: impl Into<String>, validator: ValidationFn) -> Self {\n214\t        Self {\n215\t            field_pattern: field_pattern.into(),\n216\t            validator,\n217\t        }\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    /// Check if this hook applies to the given field\n221\t    ///\n222\t    /// # Arguments\n223\t    ///\n224\t    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n225\t    ///\n226\t    /// # Returns\n227\t    ///\n228\t    /// `true` if the field pattern matches, `false` otherwise\n229\t    pub fn applies_to(&self, field: &str) -> bool {\n230\t        if self.field_pattern == \"*\" {\n231\t            return true;\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        if self.field_pattern.ends_with('*') {\n235\t            let prefix = &self.field_pattern[..self.field_pattern.len() - 1];\n236\t            return field.starts_with(prefix);\n237\t        }\n238\t\n239\t        self.field_pattern == field\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Invoke the validator\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Calls the underlying validation function with the provided inputs.\n245\t    ///\n246\t    /// # Arguments\n247\t    ///\n248\t    /// * `field` - Field name being validated\n249\t    /// * `value` - Parsed value to validate\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// # Returns\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Validation passed\n254\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Validation error message\n255\t    pub fn validate(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n256\t        (self.validator)(field, value)\n257\t    }\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\timpl fmt::Debug for ValidationHook {\n261\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n262\t        f.debug_struct(\"ValidationHook\")\n263\t            .field(\"field_pattern\", &self.field_pattern)\n264\t            .field(\"validator\", &\"<function>\")\n265\t            .finish()\n266\t    }\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// Comprehensive parser configuration\n270\t///\n271\t/// This structure consolidates all configuration options for parsing behavior.\n272\t/// It provides fine-grained control over parsing, validation, and type construction.\n273\t///\n274\t/// # Default Configuration\n275\t///\n276\t/// The default configuration is:\n277\t///\n278\t/// ```ignore\n279\t/// ParserConfig {\n280\t///     mode: Lenient,\n281\t///     allow_duplicates: true,\n282\t///     preserve_comments: false,\n283\t///     preserve_quotes: false,\n284\t///     max_depth: 0,  // unlimited\n285\t///     strict_types: false,\n286\t///     .. }\n287\t/// ```\n288\t///\n289\t/// # Examples\n290\t///\n291\t/// ## Using Defaults\n292\t///\n293\t/// ```\n294\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n295\t///\n296\t/// let config = ParserConfig::default();\n297\t/// ```\n298\t///\n299\t/// ## Strict Mode\n300\t///\n301\t/// ```\n302\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n303\t///\n304\t/// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n305\t/// ```\n306\t///\n307\t/// ## Builder Pattern\n308\t///\n309\t/// ```\n310\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n311\t///\n312\t/// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n313\t///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n314\t///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n315\t///     .max_depth(10)\n316\t///     .build();\n317\t/// ```\n318\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n319\tpub struct ParserConfig {\n320\t    /// Parsing mode (strict vs lenient)\n321\t    pub mode: ParserMode,\n322\t    /// Allow duplicate keys in mappings\n323\t    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n324\t    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n325\t    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n326\t    /// Preserve quote information in parsed strings\n327\t    pub preserve_quotes: bool,\n328\t    /// Maximum nesting depth (0 = unlimited)\n329\t    pub max_depth: usize,\n330\t    /// Enforce strict type checking (no implicit coercion)\n331\t    pub strict_types: bool,\n332\t    /// Custom type constructors registered by field name\n333\t    pub type_constructors: HashMap<String, TypeConstructor>,\n334\t    /// Custom validation hooks\n335\t    pub validation_hooks: Vec<ValidationHook>,\n336\t    /// Emit warnings for recoverable errors\n337\t    pub emit_warnings: bool,\n338\t    /// Treat warnings as errors (fail on warnings)\n339\t    pub warnings_as_errors: bool,\n340\t}\n341\t\n342\timpl Default for ParserConfig {\n343\t    fn default() -> Self {\n344\t        Self {\n345\t            mode: ParserMode::default(),\n346\t            allow_duplicates: true,\n347\t            preserve_comments: false,\n348\t            preserve_quotes: false,\n349\t            max_depth: 0,\n350\t            strict_types: false,\n351\t            type_constructors: HashMap::new(),\n352\t            validation_hooks: Vec::new(),\n353\t            emit_warnings: false,\n354\t            warnings_as_errors: false,\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t}\n358\t\n359\timpl ParserConfig {\n360\t    /// Create a builder for constructing ParserConfig\n361\t /// for strict validation. Equivalent to:\n379\t    ///\n380\t    /// ```ignore\n381\t    /// ParserConfig::builder()\n382\t    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n383\t    ///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n384\t    ///     .strict_types(true)\n385\t    ///     .build()\n386\t    /// ```\n387\t    ///\n388\t    /// # Examples\n389\t    ///\n390\t    /// ```\n391\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n394\t    /// assert!(config.mode.is_strict());\n395\t    /// assert!(!config.allow_duplicates);\n396\t    /// assert!(config.strict_types);\n397\t    /// ```\n398\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n399\t        Self {\n400\t            mode: ParserMode::Strict,\n401\t            allow_duplicates: false,\n402\t            strict_types: true,\n403\t            ..Default::default()\n404\t        }\n405\t    }\n406\t\n407\t    /// Create a lenient-mode configuration\n408\t    ///\n409\t    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n410\t    /// for forgiving parsing.            strict_types: false,\n435\t            ..Default::default()\n436\t        }\n437\t    }\n438\t\n439\t    /// Check if strict mode is enabled\n440\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool }\n448\t\n449\t    /// Register a custom type constructor\n450\t    ///\n451\t    /// # Arguments\n452\t    ///\n453\t    /// * `field` - Field name this constructor handles\n454\t    /// * constructor);\n471\t    }\n472\t\n473\t    /// Register a custom validation hook\n474\t    ///\n475\t    /// # Arguments\n476\t    ///\n477\t    /// * `hook` - The validation hook to register\n478\t    ///\n479\t    /// # Examples\n480\t    ///\n481\t    /// ```\n482\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ValidationHook};\n483\t    ///\n484\t    /// fn validate_port(field: &str, v: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n485\t    ///     // Implementation...\n486\t    ///     Ok(())\n487\t    /// }\n488\t    ///\n489\t    /// let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n490\t    /// config.register_validation(ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port));\n491\t    /// ```\n492\t    pub fn register_validation(&mut self, hook: ValidationHook) {\n493\t        self.validation_hooks.push(hook);\n494\t    }\n495\t\n496\t    /// Get type constructor for a field (if registered)\n497\t    ///\n498\t    /// # Arguments\n499\t    ///\n500\t    /// * `field` - Field name to look up\n501\t    ///\n502\t    /// # Returns\n503\t    ///\n504\t    /// `Some(&TypeConstructor)` if registered, `None` otherwise\n505\t    pub fn get_constructor(&self, field: &str) -> Arguments\n512\t    ///\n513\t    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n514\t    ///\n515\t    /// # Returns\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// Iterator over all validation hooks that apply to this |hook| hook.applies_to(field))\n522\t    }\n523\t}\n524\t\n525\t/// Builder for constructing ParserConfig instances\n526\t///\n527\t/// Provides a fluent interface for creating parser }\n553\t    }\n554\t\n555\t    /// Set the parsing mode\n556\t    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: ParserMode) -> Self {\n557\t        self.config.mode = mode;\n558\t        self\n559\t    }\n560\t\n561\t
